name: Germany (merchant flag, 1946–1949)

colors.symbolism: the outer bands of the international 'C' signal flag
the inner bands of the 'C' signal flag
the central band of the 'C' signal flag

description: The international maritime 'C' signal flag — five horizontal bands of blue, white, red, white, blue — with a triangular swallowtail cut taken from the fly.
history: After World War II the Allies denied defeated Germany the use of any national or merchant flag. Allied Control Council Law No. 39 (1946) imposed the so-called C-Pennant or C-Doppelstander, the international code signal flag for the letter 'C' with a triangle cut from the fly, as the compulsory ensign of German merchant ships. It served until 1949–1950, when German vessels were again permitted to fly the black-red-gold flag.

features.symbolism: the international 'C' signal flag imposed as a mark of occupation
